name: core
description: v2.8.0 ships codex-sutra v1.0.0 — Sutra-owned codex CLI wrapper at marketplace/plugin/skills/codex-sutra/SKILL.md (540 lines). Three founder-driven changes vs gstack /codex (the prior interim primitive): (1) hard cap raised 5m → 15m to fit high-effort reviews on medium diffs, enforced via wrapper-side polling on a process-group kill (kill -TERM -PGID with setsid / os.setsid fallback) — Bash foreground caps at 10m, so 15m requires bg + polling; (2) fail-closed semantics for non-model failures (codex crash, malformed output, log-write failure, hard-cap timeout) with structured `reason` codes + three-channel result durability (gate-log.jsonl primary, /tmp fallback file, stderr CODEX-SUTRA-RESULT beacon) so PROTO-019 hooks always have an observable verdict; (3) four documented modes — review (P1/P2 gate), challenge (adversarial), consult (with .context/codex-session-id continuity), design-review (single-file review used during this very release for v1→v2→v3 self-review). Skill ships with explicit defense-in-depth (codex sandboxed via -s read-only -C; prompt-level filesystem boundary; output scan post-hoc), single-writer rule for $TMPDONE (eliminates v2 race), stdin closed via </dev/null (codex exec hangs without it — discovered during v2 design review), 24h orphan reaping for /tmp/codex-sutra-* artifacts, and a documented quarterly upstream-sync cadence with gstack /codex. Rollout staged T2 owned (DayFlow/Billu/Paisa/PPR/Maze) week 1 → T3 projects (Testlify/Dharmik) week 2 → T4 fleet week 3+, gated on zero infra-fail verdicts in gate-log.jsonl. v2.8.0 also wires PROTO-019 user-facing messages (codex-directive-gate.sh, codex-review-gate.sh) from /codex to /codex-sutra (5 line changes across 3 hooks) and fixes codex-directive-detect.sh false-positive bug where the regex matched its own task-notification XML echoes — new perl-based system-XML strip step (allowlist: task-notification, system-reminder, command-name, command-message, command-args, local-command-stdout, local-command-stderr) with graceful fallback when perl unavailable. 6/6 unit tests pass; pre-existing official test suite has v2-vs-v3 marker-path rot (2026-04-25 transition) tracked separately. Codex review chain for design: v1 CHANGES-REQUIRED → v2 CHANGES-REQUIRED → v3 ADVISORY → ship; for hooks: v1 CHANGES-REQUIRED → v2 PASS → delta PASS. Verdict files at .enforcement/codex-reviews/2026-04-28-*. v2.7.1 actually applies the assistant-observer.sh B7+B9 fix that v2.7.0's CURRENT-VERSION.md described as shipped but whose code change was missed during the v2.7.0 commit. The narrative in the v2.6.3/v2.7.0 sections was forward-referencing a working-tree edit that never made it into the commit. v2.7.1 closes the gap: drops the redundant nested ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} from the HOOK_LOG default value (closing B7 path-double-substitution + B9 set-u crash with one edit), and switches the event evidence_refs[0].source field from `${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/holding/hooks/hook-log.jsonl` to `$HOOK_LOG` so the SUTRA_ASSISTANT_HOOK_LOG override is reflected accurately. Mirrored to holding/hooks/assistant-observer.sh in the holding repo. 24/24 holding/hooks/tests/test-assistant-observer.sh tests green. v2.7.0 streamlines the permission system per codex consult 2026-04-28: removes ~50% of code (1366 → 690 LoC) without changing threat model or behavior. DELETED: lib/sh_lex_check.py (228 LoC v2.4 compositional matcher — Trust Mode is a strict superset for Bash); scripts/rollback-compositional.sh; 3 obsolete test files; _match_bash_compositional + _env_has_shadowing in permission-gate.sh (~50 LoC); allowlist mirror + env-shadowing + Haiku LLM path in bash-summary-pretool.sh (~210 LoC). REFACTORED: bash-summary-pretool.sh is now a 152-LoC thin wrapper that calls sh_trust_mode.py once and emits one of 6 deterministic category labels (no LLM cost, equivalent UX). Single source of truth: every hook calls the same classifier, eliminating drift bugs codex caught (rtk * and claude plugin update * skipped by bash-summary but not by permission-gate). Tier 1 retained for Write/Edit/MultiEdit + plugin-lifecycle Bash per codex correction. 156/156 trust-mode unit tests green. Threat model unchanged (single trusted local operator). v2.6.3 closes B7+B9 in assistant-observer.sh (HOOK_LOG line) — single edit fixes both. B7: prior `${SUTRA_ASSISTANT_HOOK_LOG:-$REPO_ROOT/${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/holding/hooks/hook-log.jsonl}` double-substituted REPO_ROOT and an absolute CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR producing an invalid path that silently no-op'd. B9 (discovered 2026-04-28 verifying v2.6.2 deployed shim): same line crashed under `set -uo pipefail` when CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR unset (manual-shell opt-in path). Fix: drop the redundant nested CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR — REPO_ROOT alone is the correct base; HOOK_LOG override path preserved. Event src field switched to `$HOOK_LOG` for accuracy when SUTRA_ASSISTANT_HOOK_LOG override is used. Mirrored to holding/hooks/assistant-observer.sh. Re-enable pre-conditions (assistant layer, paused per D37): B1, B5, B6, B8 remain — B2/B3/B4 fixed in v2.6.2, B7+B9 fixed here. v2.6.2 ships D36+D37 (founder directives 2026-04-27, decoupled by mechanism). D36 Feedback Channel three-layer defense after a real client session bypassed /core:feedback --public via direct `gh issue create` when the binary failed on a non-existent `feedback,vunknown` label: (a) routing-rule.sh strengthened with explicit no-gh-fallback clause during binary error recovery; (b) NEW feedback-channel-guard.sh PreToolUse:Bash hook blocks `gh (issue|pr) (create|comment|review)` against any sankalpasawa/sutra* repo with exit 2 (12/12 tests; SUTRA_FEEDBACK_GUARD_DISABLED=1 kill-switch); (c) feedback.sh binary --label flag dropped, version moved to title `[feedback v${PLUGIN_VERSION}] from plugin`. D37 Sutra Assistant Layer: paused, default-OFF — polarity flipped in assistant-kill-switch.sh, opt-in via ~/.sutra-assistant-enabled or SUTRA_ASSISTANT_ENABLED; hard kill-switches still win. bin/sutra `enable`/`disable` rewritten. Bug list B1-B8 captured in holding/research/2026-04-24-assistant-layer-design.md §0 — re-enable requires clearing those before `sutra enable`. v2.6.1 refines Trust Mode `gh`/`git` to a catastrophic-only rule per founder direction ("keep it simple — only delete"): `gh` removed from REMOTE_TOOLS, prompts only on delete-class actions (`gh repo delete`, `gh secret delete`/`remove`, `gh release delete`, `gh issue delete`, `gh codespace delete`, `gh label delete`, `gh extension remove`, `gh gist delete`, `gh variable delete`); `git` simplified to prompt only on `push --force`/`-f`/`--force-with-lease` and `clean -f*`. Everything else (gh pr create/edit/merge/close/comment, issue create/comment, repo create/edit/fork, secret set, auth login/logout, workflow run, gh api any-method; git commit/push/pull/rebase/merge/reset --hard/checkout/branch -D/tag -d/stash drop/rm/mv) auto-approves — all recoverable via reflog, remote, or fs. 156/156 unit tests green. Supersedes the initial v2.6.1 granular spec (36431a8) per 16af031. v2.6.0 ships PROTO-024 V1 client→team feedback fanout: /core:feedback now scrubs locally and pushes to sankalpasawa/sutra-data clients/<install_id>/feedback/ (collaborator-visible inbox, V2 will encrypt). Strengthened scrub_text() with 12+ token detectors (GitHub/OpenAI/AWS/Slack/Stripe/JWT/signed-URL/DSN/webhook/email/phone) plus 40+ char high-entropy fallback. Decoupled /core:feedback from SUTRA_TELEMETRY=0. push.sh stops writing manifest.identity (closes v1.9 PII leak). reset-turn-markers.sh moved from UserPromptSubmit→Stop event (closes spoof vulnerability — no content pattern detection needed). Codex review 2026-04-25 directive 1777062127+1777058308. Governance preserved end-to-end. v2.5.0 adds Tier 1.6 Trust Mode (denylist of 6 prompt categories: git mutations, privilege, recursive delete outside safe paths, disk/system, fetch-and-exec, remote/shared-state). Auto-approves everything else. Per founder approval-fatigue feedback + Codex MODIFY->GO. Operating system for building with AI. v2.1.1 patch (fleet feedback + codex review 2026-04-25): (a) /core:start project-root guard — refuses activation in $HOME or non-project dirs (no .git / package.json / pyproject.toml / Cargo.toml / go.mod / CLAUDE.md), canonical path compare prevents symlink/trailing-slash bypass, .git-as-file recognized for worktrees/submodules. --force override. Prevents home-dir poisoning of ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md with project-scoped governance. Covered by tests/unit/test-start-guard.sh (10 assertions). (b) /core:feedback docs synced to v2.1 reality — --public flag to gh CLI documented (was already wired but commands/feedback.md + usage help still said 'NOT YET IMPLEMENTED'). (c) hooks/reset-turn-markers.sh: root-cause fix — treats empty PROMPT as synthetic turn (was falling through to wipe, caused 2052:1 wipe:skip ratio blocking multi-tool Sutra edits). v2.0 — privacy model replaced: signals-not-content capture, consent-gated disk writes, T4 in-memory-default. User surface = single PRIVACY.md sheet + SUTRA_TELEMETRY=0 opt-out + /sutra feedback manual channel. Legacy v1.9 identity stamping + outbound push DEPRECATED, gated behind SUTRA_LEGACY_TELEMETRY=1. Governance surface preserved: input routing, depth estimation, readability gate, output trace, session-retrieve, per-profile enforcement, C3c token-compression, permission-gate, Bash prompt summarizer, feedback-routing-rule. Codex-reviewed DIRECTIVE-ID 1777065370 (CHANGES-REQUIRED → all P1/P2 findings absorbed).

profile.title: Enforcement profile
profile.description: individual = warn-only, telemetry off. project = warn-only, telemetry on (default). company = HARD block on missing depth markers, telemetry on.
